key_insight: A repeatable two-part pour-casting workflow and sensor embedding method enable robust fabrication of soft pneumatic actuators with reliable bending response.
engineering_relevance: This gives soft robotics researchers and manufacturers a practical, scalable route to build airtight actuators and integrate sensing for control and validation.
method: Use two-part pour casting with procedures that prevent cavity clogging and improve airtight sealing.
Embed thin-film flex sensors using a repeatable placement and encapsulation workflow.
Validate the actuator with FEM, PID pressure-controlled actuation, and automated image-based angle calibration.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports repeatable tests across two operators and 24 successful fabrications, with staircase and sinusoidal experiments showing comparable simulated and measured bending behavior.
watch_item: Thin-film flex sensing and pneumatic soft actuators can still show viscoelastic hysteresis, so calibration and operator consistency remain important.
paper_title: Robust Silicone Pour Casting and Sensor Embedding Procedures for Soft Robotic Actuators
